title: Integrate ffmpeg-static and create server-side audio splitter helper
description: Install `ffmpeg-static` and `fluent-ffmpeg` or write a custom native execution helper in Next.js backend to extract and slice audio files into sequential 30-second WAV/MP3 files.
acceptanceCriteria: `ffmpeg-static` package is successfully installed in package.json.
A server-side helper function takes a media file path and splits it into 30-second segments in a temporary directory.
Segments are saved in the correct numerical order (e.g. `chunk_0.wav`, `chunk_1.wav`).

steps.details: Run `npm i ffmpeg-static` and `@types/fluent-ffmpeg` to configure packaging.
Create `src/lib/audioSplitter.ts` using `child_process.exec` or `fluent-ffmpeg` to slice files. The path to the static binary is imported from `ffmpeg-static`.
Write a lightweight test script in `scratch/` folder to split a dummy test file locally and verify slices are created correctly.

technicalNotes: Make sure to set file paths and directories dynamically using `path` library. Delete temporary directories on finish.
